channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Flaky Tests You Can't Fix With Better Selectors
item.description: You've fixed your locators. You've switched to web-first assertions. Your tests still fail intermittently — but now the failures look different. Duplicate records in the database. Tests that pass...

item.title: Send your first AI message in one API call
item.description: Most AI tutorials start with a setup checklist. Pick a model provider. Create an account. Wire up a vector database for memory. Stand up a server to hold conversation state. Glue it all together....

item.title: Add Peppol e-invoicing to your SaaS — the easy way to get ahead of the EU mandates
item.description: When you're building a young B2B SaaS, Peppol e-invoicing lives in the "we'll deal with it later" pile. Invoicing is a feature, not your product. No customer has demanded it yet. And the whole...

item.title: AI Coding Tools for Machine Learning Engineers in 2026: Jupyter, PyTorch, and the CUDA Trap
item.description: This article was originally published on aicoderscope.com ML engineers aren't software engineers who happen to write some Python. They live in notebooks, build training loops, fight CUDA...

item.title: Claude API from Scratch: Your First Working Call in 30 Minutes (2026)
item.description: This article was originally published on aicoderscope.com Most Claude API tutorials start in the middle. They assume you already have a key configured, a virtual environment ready, and a working...

item.title: AI coding tools for TypeScript and JavaScript developers in 2026: who actually handles generics, JSX trees, and the module resolution mess?
item.description: This article was originally published on aicoderscope.com TL;DR: Cursor Pro ($20/month) is the strongest daily driver for TypeScript/React multi-file work; GitHub Copilot Pro ($10/month) wins on...

item.title: OpenCode review 2026: the open-source terminal coding agent that challenges Claude Code
item.description: This article was originally published on aicoderscope.com TL;DR: OpenCode is an MIT-licensed, provider-agnostic terminal coding agent with 167k GitHub stars built by the team behind terminal.shop....

item.title: O Marketing do Apocalipse: Como a Ameaça à Democracia Virou a Vaga de Emprego Mais Cobiçada de 2026 no Mercado de T.I.?
item.description: No saturado mercado de tecnologia, as empresas gastam fortunas tentando convencer o público de que seus produtos vão salvar o mundo. A Anthropic decidiu adotar a estratégia oposta. Ao admitir...

item.title: Using one LLM API key across OpenAI-compatible clients, Claude Code, and terminal agents
item.description: If you test multiple LLM clients, the annoying part is rarely the first API call. It is the mess around it: keys, base URLs, model names, billing pages, and protocol differences. TokenHub is a...

item.title: AI Pipeline: Preventing Drift in Production Systems
item.description: A common failure pattern in a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system is a progressive decline in performance. This decline, which can be difficult for users to detect initially, often begins...

item.title: Microsoft created the mini Surface dev box that Qualcomm couldn’t
item.description: Microsoft only just announced a new Surface Laptop Ultra at the weekend, and it's now revealing a miniature Surface PC aimed at developers. The new Surface RTX Spark Dev Box is powered by Nvidia's...

item.title: Microsoft’s new developer-optimized Windows embraces Linux even more
item.description: Microsoft is kicking off its Build developer conference today with a promise of making Windows a trusted platform for development. As the company continues to focus on performance and reliability...

item.title: Trump goes after green cards
item.description: On the Friday before Memorial Day, on the eve of a long weekend, the Trump administration announced that it was further gutting legal immigration. The Department of Homeland Security didn't use...

item.title: Trump signs narrower executive order on AI oversight after industry objections
item.description: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.

item.title: Cricut’s Joy 2 helps you personalize Father’s Day gifts, and it’s at its lowest price yet
item.description: If you’re looking for a way to add a personal touch to Father’s Day or graduation gifts, the Cricut Joy 2 can help you customize everything from water bottles and bookmarks to greeting cards and...

item.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
item.description: OpenAI is getting serious about courting enterprise users. On Tuesday, the AI lab released a new set of capabilities for Codex, meant to expand the agentic tool’s uses in the workplace. Together...

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark, and more
item.description: Microsoft’s annual developer conference is kicking off on June 2nd in San Francisco with the keynote presentation streaming live at 12:30PM ET / 9:30AM PT, and we will be following along here with...

item.title: Ableton is letting musicians build browser-style extensions for Live
item.description: Ableton already has Max for Live, which allows users to build MIDI effects, synths, and samplers for its digital audio workstation (DAW). But now with its Extensions SDK, features can be added to...

item.title: Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget
item.description: Opal, the company famous for making a fancy webcam, has pivoted to making other consumer electronics. Fueled by big investments from OpenAI and Samsung, it’s working on an audio gadget first.

item.title: The screenless Camp Snap 2 is slimmer and comes with more filters
item.description: After expanding its offerings to video with the CS-8 inspired by Kodak and Canon's retro Super 8mm film cameras, Camp Snap is returning to its roots. The Camp Snap 2 is a sequel to the company's...

item.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
item.description: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water,...

item.title: Holo3.1: Fast & Local Computer Use Agents
Lego’s Smart Play Pokémon can train and battle, but don’t do the one thing I wish they could
item.description: When Lego announced its tech-packed Smart Bricks at CES, we were impressed by the potential - enough to give it our Best in Show award. But when the first Star Wars sets actually launched in...

item.title: Thermacell’s latest smart mosquito system is bigger and more expensive
item.description: Thermacell has launched Liv 2.0, the next generation of its Wi-Fi-connected smart mosquito protection system. It features new hardware and can cover a larger area, and Thermacell says its formula...

item.title: Amazon’s four-day Prime Day begins on June 23rd
item.description: Amazon bucked its usual tradition of having Prime Day in July. Prime Day 2026 is happening in June, kicking off in just a few weeks. Prime members will get access to many deals starting June 23rd...

item.title: ZeroDrift raises $10M to protect AI models from themselves
item.description: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.

item.title: The Download: AI can run your admin department now
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How small businesses can leverage AI From accounting to...

item.title: Rocket engine startup Impulse raises $500 million to hire people, not AI
item.description: Engineering physical systems still depends on human talent, according to Impulse Space president Eric Romo.

item.title: Travelers deploys AI-powered claims countrywide with OpenAI
item.description: Travelers built an AI-powered Claim Assistant with OpenAI to guide customers through filing claims, provide 24/7 support, and scale operations during peak demand.

item.title: AI-Driven Exploitation is Destroying Vulnerability Management. Here’s How to Handle It.
item.description: AI-driven exploitation timelines are rapidly shrinking, and they are not going to stop shrinking. Vulnerabilities are being discovered, reproduced, and weaponized faster than ever in the history...
